content.problemStatement:
**This is the easy version of the problem. It is worth 252525 points in division 111, and 100100100 points in all other divisions.**

For an array BBB of length MMM, you are allowed to perform the following operation as many times as required:


Let S=∑i=1MBiS = \sum\limits_{i=1}^M{B_i}S=i=1∑M​Bi​
Pick iii such that 1≤i≤min⁡(M,∣S∣)1 \leq i \leq \min(M,|S|)1≤i≤min(M,∣S∣)
Either increment or decrement BiB_iBi​ by 111
Define f(B)f(B)f(B) to be the minimum number of operations needed to turn every element of BBB to 000, or −1-1−1 if it's impossible to achieve.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN. Output the value f(A)f(A)f(A).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the number of elements of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers — the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the value f(A)f(A)f(A).
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 222: One sequence of four operations is:

Decrement A1A_1A1​ by 111, to get [2,0,1][2, 0, 1][2,0,1]. The sum is now 333.
Decrement A3A_3A3​ by 111, to get [2,0,0][2, 0, 0][2,0,0]. The sum is now 222.
Decrement A1A_1A1​ by 111, to get [1,0,0][1, 0, 0][1,0,0]. The sum is now 111.
Decrement A1A_1A1​ by 111, to get [0,0,0][0, 0, 0][0,0,0].

Test case 333: The array is already filled with zeros.
Test case 555: It can be shown that it's impossible to make every element 000.
